---
title: "07 — DiD Estimation via fect"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, message = TRUE, warning = TRUE)
knitr::opts_knit$set(root.dir = "C:/Users/ammonsj/Ideas")
source("C:/Users/ammonsj/Ideas/_config.R")
```

## Load panel

```{r load}
library(arrow)
library(data.table)
library(fect)
library(panelView)
library(ggplot2)

panel <- as.data.table(read_parquet(
  file.path(DATA_PANELS, "did_panel.parquet")
))

message("Panel: ", nrow(panel), " rows, ",
        uniqueN(panel$newspaper_id), " newspapers, ",
        uniqueN(panel$time_id), " time periods")
message("Treated newspaper-months: ", sum(panel$treated))
message("Control newspaper-months: ", sum(panel$treated == 0))
```

## Visualize treatment pattern

```{r panelview, fig.width=10, fig.height=8}
# panelView expects a formula: Y ~ D
# Use a subset for visualization if panel is very large
set.seed(SEED)
if (uniqueN(panel$newspaper_id) > 200) {
  # Sample 100 treated + 100 control newspapers
  treated_nps <- unique(panel[treated == 1, newspaper_id])
  control_nps <- unique(panel[treated == 0 & !(newspaper_id %in% treated_nps), newspaper_id])

  sample_nps <- c(
    sample(treated_nps, min(100, length(treated_nps))),
    sample(control_nps, min(100, length(control_nps)))
  )
  panel_viz <- panel[newspaper_id %in% sample_nps]
} else {
  panel_viz <- panel
}

panelview(incivil_rate_rf ~ treated,
          data = as.data.frame(panel_viz),
          index = c("newspaper_id", "time_id"),
          type = "treat",
          main = "Treatment Status: Coughlin Exposure Across Newspapers")
```

## Model 1: Interactive Fixed Effects (IFEct) — Primary

```{r ife}
message("\n=== Interactive Fixed Effects (IFE) ===")

out_ife <- fect(
  incivil_rate_rf ~ treated,
  data = as.data.frame(panel),
  index = c("newspaper_id", "time_id"),
  method = "ife",
  force = "two-way",
  se = TRUE,
  vartype = "bootstrap",
  nboots = 200,
  parallel = TRUE,
  cores = max(1, parallel::detectCores() - 1),
  seed = SEED
)

print(out_ife)

# Save model
saveRDS(out_ife, file.path(MODELS_DIR, "fect_ife.rds"))
```

## Model 2: Matrix Completion

```{r mc}
message("\n=== Matrix Completion ===")

out_mc <- fect(
  incivil_rate_rf ~ treated,
  data = as.data.frame(panel),
  index = c("newspaper_id", "time_id"),
  method = "mc",
  se = TRUE,
  vartype = "bootstrap",
  nboots = 200,
  parallel = TRUE,
  cores = max(1, parallel::detectCores() - 1),
  seed = SEED
)

print(out_mc)
saveRDS(out_mc, file.path(MODELS_DIR, "fect_mc.rds"))
```

## Model 3: Two-Way Fixed Effects (benchmark)

```{r fe}
message("\n=== Two-Way Fixed Effects ===")

out_fe <- fect(
  incivil_rate_rf ~ treated,
  data = as.data.frame(panel),
  index = c("newspaper_id", "time_id"),
  method = "fe",
  force = "two-way",
  se = TRUE,
  vartype = "bootstrap",
  nboots = 200,
  parallel = TRUE,
  cores = max(1, parallel::detectCores() - 1),
  seed = SEED
)

print(out_fe)
saveRDS(out_fe, file.path(MODELS_DIR, "fect_fe.rds"))
```

## Model 4: Placebo (pre-trend) test

```{r placebo}
message("\n=== Placebo Test ===")

out_placebo <- fect(
  incivil_rate_rf ~ treated,
  data = as.data.frame(panel),
  index = c("newspaper_id", "time_id"),
  method = "ife",
  force = "two-way",
  se = TRUE,
  vartype = "bootstrap",
  nboots = 200,
  parallel = TRUE,
  cores = max(1, parallel::detectCores() - 1),
  seed = SEED,
  placeboTest = TRUE,
  placebo.period = c(-6, -1)
)

print(out_placebo)
saveRDS(out_placebo, file.path(MODELS_DIR, "fect_placebo.rds"))
```

## Model 5: Carryover test (equilibrium shift / persistence)

```{r carryover}
message("\n=== Carryover Test (Persistence) ===")

out_carry <- fect(
  incivil_rate_rf ~ treated,
  data = as.data.frame(panel),
  index = c("newspaper_id", "time_id"),
  method = "ife",
  force = "two-way",
  se = TRUE,
  vartype = "bootstrap",
  nboots = 200,
  parallel = TRUE,
  cores = max(1, parallel::detectCores() - 1),
  seed = SEED,
  carryoverTest = TRUE,
  carryover.period = c(1, 12)
)

print(out_carry)
saveRDS(out_carry, file.path(MODELS_DIR, "fect_carryover.rds"))
```

## Robustness: Lexicon-based outcome

```{r robustness-lexicon}
message("\n=== Robustness: Lexicon-based outcome ===")

out_ife_lex <- fect(
  incivil_rate_lexicon ~ treated,
  data = as.data.frame(panel),
  index = c("newspaper_id", "time_id"),
  method = "ife",
  force = "two-way",
  se = TRUE,
  vartype = "bootstrap",
  nboots = 200,
  parallel = TRUE,
  cores = max(1, parallel::detectCores() - 1),
  seed = SEED
)

print(out_ife_lex)
saveRDS(out_ife_lex, file.path(MODELS_DIR, "fect_ife_lexicon.rds"))
```

## Results summary

```{r results-summary}
# Extract ATT estimates
extract_att <- function(model, label) {
  att <- model$att.avg
  se <- model$att.avg.se
  data.table(
    Model = label,
    ATT = round(att, 4),
    SE = round(se, 4),
    CI_lower = round(att - 1.96 * se, 4),
    CI_upper = round(att + 1.96 * se, 4),
    p_value = round(2 * pnorm(-abs(att / se)), 4)
  )
}

results <- rbindlist(list(
  extract_att(out_ife, "IFE (primary)"),
  extract_att(out_mc, "Matrix Completion"),
  extract_att(out_fe, "Two-Way FE"),
  extract_att(out_ife_lex, "IFE (lexicon outcome)")
))

print(results)

# Save results table
tex <- knitr::kable(results, format = "latex", booktabs = TRUE,
                    caption = "Average Treatment Effect on the Treated (ATT) Estimates",
                    col.names = c("Model", "ATT", "SE", "CI Lower", "CI Upper", "p-value"))
save_table <- function(tex_string, filename) {
  for (dir in c(TABLES_DIR, OVERLEAF_TABLES)) {
    writeLines(tex_string, file.path(dir, paste0(filename, ".tex")))
  }
}
save_table(tex, "main_results")
message("Saved main results table.")
```
